chunk_summary: This section discusses India-specific referral pathways for neurodisability and feeding support through RBSK and DEIC, emphasizing practical strategies for improving access to therapy, nutrition, and rehabilitation services for children with dysphagia and related conditions.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of RBSK and DEIC in supporting children with neurodisability and feeding challenges in India.
Learn how to create effective referral plans for dysphagia and nutrition support within the Indian healthcare framework.
Identify key clinic actions to improve follow-through for feeding and swallowing interventions.
Address India-specific feasibility issues such as caregiver literacy, travel constraints, and access to feeding supplies.
key_facts: RBSK screens children from birth to 18 years for selected conditions and provides referral and tracking support.
DEICs focus on early childhood services (up to 6 years) and link older children to rehabilitation and tertiary care.
Explicit referral reasons, such as 'unsafe swallowing with recurrent chest infections,' improve follow-through.
School-identified children should be referred via the nearest PHC/CHC into the DEIC network to reduce family burden.
Referral notes for higher-centre swallow studies should include decision hinges like 'tube-weaning readiness' or 'safest liquid level for hydration.'
tags_mesh: Feeding and Eating Disorders of Childhood
reference_quote: Under RBSK, children from birth to 18 years can be screened for selected conditions, linked to confirmation, early intervention, referral support, and tracking. (Source: RBSK Operational Guidelines)
document_key_topics: Role of RBSK and DEIC in feeding support
